key_strengths: National HDI has steadily improved from 0.736 in 1995 to 0.758 in 2003.
High adult literacy and combined enrollment rates relative to global peers.
Strong human development outcomes in leading Luzon provinces like Benguet and Cavite.
Active civil society and indigenous mechanisms pushing for grassroots peace-building.
key_challenges: Protracted Moro and communist insurgencies causing massive economic and human losses.
Severe regional disparities, with ARMM provinces lagging in all development metrics.
High levels of internal displacement (IDPs) and loss of cultural identity.
Widespread latent anti-Muslim prejudice among the mainstream population.

evaluation.critique: Extraction adhered perfectly to the requested schema. Extracted metrics map perfectly to time-series trend requirements.
